nativeRange.habitat: Bucephalandra 'Bukit Kelam' is named after Bukit Kelam, a prominent granite inselberg in West Kalimantan, Indonesian Borneo, near where the parent species Bucephalandra pygmaea was originally collected. Like all Bucephalandra, it grows naturally as a rheophyte — a plant adapted to strong current — anchored to rocks and submerged wood along fast-flowing rainforest streams, periodically exposed and resubmerged as seasonal water levels shift.

decorPreferences: lava rock, driftwood, or porous hardscape to attach the rhizome to
moderate flow across the rhizome to mimic Borneo rheophyte streams
low to moderate lighting; tolerates shaded foreground or midground placement
stable, established tank — avoid newly cycled systems

goodWith: Neocaridina and Caridina shrimp which graze biofilm without harming the plant
other low-light epiphytes such as Anubias, Java fern and mosses
avoidWith: large herbivorous or rhizome-disturbing fish such as goldfish, silver dollars and large plecos
vigorous diggers (large cichlids) that may dislodge attached rhizomes
newly set up tanks with unstable parameters

summary: Bucephalandra 'Bukit Kelam' is a compact cultivar of Bucephalandra pygmaea, named after a granite mountain in West Kalimantan, Borneo. It produces small, wavy-edged dark to bright green leaves often speckled with tiny white dots, and is a slow-growing epiphyte suited to low-light foreground or midground positions on hardscape.
careNotes: Bucephalandra 'Bukit Kelam' is a small cultivar of Bucephalandra pygmaea, and its care follows the same principles as the broader genus [bucephalandra]. The creeping rhizome (the thick horizontal stem from which the leaves and roots emerge) must be attached to lava rock, driftwood, slate, or other hardscape using super glue gel or thread. DO NOT BURY THE RHIZOME in substrate — doing so will cause it to rot. Leaves are wavy-edged and develop characteristic small white spots or speckles on the surface, particularly in plants that have been grown above water. Lower-light placement is generally advised, though medium light commonly brings out richer color and the iridescent sheen typical of Bucephalandra. CO2 injection is not required but is generally reported to improve growth rate and coloration. Tissue-cultured stock arrives free of algae, snails, and pest hitchhikers and is widely considered the preferred source format. Like other Bucephalandra, freshly imported plants commonly experience 'buce melt' — older leaves that were grown above water drop off when the plant transitions to fully submerged conditions — but the plant typically recovers from the rhizome over several weeks when introduced into a stable, established aquarium. Water parameters follow the well-documented genus-level range: approximately 22–28 °C, pH 6.0–7.5, gH 3–15.
breedingNotes: Propagation is by vegetative rhizome division, as with all Bucephalandra. This cultivar tends to branch readily on its own without active trimming. Once a side branch has developed its own leaves and roots, cut the rhizome at a natural bend with clean scissors and attach the new division to a separate piece of hardscape.

primary.name: KeepFishKeeping
primary.notes: Primary source. Confirmed scientific name Bucephalandra pygmaea 'Bukit Kelam', tissue-cultured form, Southeast Asia rheophyte origin, leaf dimensions ~2 cm × 5 cm, slow-growing, low light preference, epiphyte (rhizome must not be buried), easy care, branches readily for propagation.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: Cross-checked scientific name (Bucephalandra pygmaea 'Bukit Kelam'), adult height ~5-10 cm (2-4 inches), slow growth, wavy-edged bright green leaves with small white-dot speckling, tissue-cultured format pest/algae free. Product page does not list quantitative water parameters.

additional.name: AquaMate genus entry (Bucephalandra)
additional.notes: Inherited husbandry parameters (temperature 22-28 °C, pH 6.0-7.5, gH 3-15, lighting/CO2/fertilization profile, rhizome attachment, buce-melt acclimation behavior) from the previously researched parent entry fw-plant-016, which cross-references Tropica, Aquarium Co-Op (genus guide), 2HR Aquarist, Aquariadise and AquariumBreeder. Both product pages for this cultivar are silent on numeric water parameters, so the genus values are applied to this cultivar by inheritance.
